caption:
The rain hit my synthetic skin like a thousand tiny needles, but I didn’t flinch. Neon light bled through the grime of Neo-Tokyo’s underbelly, painting my reflection in shades of electric violet and chrome blue. I adjusted the collar of my leather harness, feeling the familiar hum of the nano-threads against my skin.

Tonight wasn’t just another data drop. The target was a VIP client in Sector 7, a man who paid in credits, but more importantly, in whispers. My skirt clung to my thighs, the smart-fabric shifting to accentuate my curves as I moved through the crowded noodle stall. The scent of synthetic spice and ozone clung to the air.

"She’s beautiful," a voice murmured behind me. I didn’t turn, just smiled, a slow, predatory curve. My cybernetic implants buzzed softly, syncing with the rhythm of the club’s bass. Every step felt calculated, every breath measured. The leather bodysuit hugged my figure, hiding the dangerous power beneath the soft curves of my breasts and hips.

When the target approached, the crowd parted. He was a giant in a trench coat, eyes scanning for vulnerabilities. I leaned in, the heat of his breath mixing with the cool mist of the rain outside. "You’re late," I whispered, letting my hand glide down his arm, electric sparks dancing on my fingertips. The data transfer happened in a flash. My mind expanded, downloading his secrets while my body stayed calm, the fabric of my outfit straining slightly against the tension in my muscles. The crowd watched, mesmerized. I wasn’t just a courier; I was the ghost in the machine.

As he handed over the credit chip, the zipper on my thigh panel hissed softly. The crowd cheered, not just for the tech, but for the show. I winked at the camera hidden in the lens of a passing drone. The night was young, and the neon lights were just getting brighter.
